heading: Passenger manifests
label: 44909 Passenger manifests

L. 101–604, § 203(c)104 Stat. 3083In subsection (a)(1), before clause (A), the words “each air carrier” are substituted “all United States air carriers” because of the definition of “air carrier” in section 40102(a) of the revised title. The words “an appropriate representative of the Secretary of State” are substituted for “appropriate representatives of the United States Department of State” because of 22:2651 and for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code. In clause (B), the words “to comply with clause (A) of this paragraph” are substituted for “to fulfill the requirement of this subsection” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the Code.In subsection (a)(2), before clause (B), the words “For purposes of this section” are omitted as unnecessary.In subsection (a)(3), the words “In carrying out this subsection” are substituted for “In implementing the requirement pursuant to the amendment made by subsection (a) of this section” for clarity and to eliminate unnecessary words.In subsection (b), the word “imposing” is added for clarity. The words “imposed on air carriers under subsection (a)(1) and (2) of this section” are substituted for “imposed pursuant to the amendment made by subsection (a)” for clarity and because of the restatement.

children.label: A
children.text: not later than one hour after that carrier is notified of an aviation disaster outside the United States involving that flight; or

children.label: B
children.text: if it is not technologically feasible or reasonable to comply with clause (A) of this paragraph, then as expeditiously as possible, but not later than 3 hours after the carrier is so notified.

children.label: A
children.text: the full name of each passenger.

children.label: B
children.text: the passport number of each passenger, if required for travel.

children.label: C
children.text: the name and telephone number of a contact for each passenger.

In carrying out this subsection, the Secretary of Transportation shall consider the necessity and feasibility of requiring air carriers to collect passenger manifest information as a condition for passengers boarding a flight of the carrier.

children.heading: .—Foreign Air Carrier Requirements
children.label: b .—Foreign Air Carrier Requirements
children.text: The Secretary of Transportation shall consider imposing a requirement on foreign air carriers comparable to that imposed on air carriers under subsection (a)(1) and (2) of this section.

children.heading: Flights in Foreign Air Transportation to the United States.—
children.label: c Flights in Foreign Air Transportation to the United States.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Each air carrier and foreign air carrier operating a passenger flight in foreign air transportation to the United States shall provide to the Commissioner of U.S. Customs and Border Protection by electronic transmission a passenger and crew manifest containing the information specified in paragraph (2). Carriers may use the advanced passenger information system established under section 431 of the Tariff Act of 1930 () to provide the information required by the preceding sentence.19 U.S.C. 1431

children.label: A
children.text: The full name of each passenger and crew member.

children.label: B
children.text: The date of birth and citizenship of each passenger and crew member.

children.label: C
children.text: The sex of each passenger and crew member.

children.label: D
children.text: The passport number and country of issuance of each passenger and crew member if required for travel.

children.label: E
children.text: The United States visa number or resident alien card number of each passenger and crew member, as applicable.

children.label: F
children.text: Such other information as the Administrator of the Transportation Security Administration, in consultation with the Commissioner of U.S. Customs and Border Protection, determines is reasonably necessary to ensure aviation safety.

children.heading: .—Passenger name records
children.label: 3 .—Passenger name records
children.text: The carriers shall make passenger name record information available to the Customs Service upon request.

children.heading: .—Transmission of manifest
children.label: 4 .—Transmission of manifest
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (5) and (6), a passenger and crew manifest required for a flight under paragraph (1) shall be transmitted to the Customs Service in advance of the aircraft landing in the United States in such manner, time, and form as the Customs Service prescribes.

children.heading: .—Transmission of manifests to other federal agencies
children.label: 5 .—Transmission of manifests to other federal agencies
children.text: Upon request, information provided to the Administrator of the Transportation Security Administration or the Customs Service under this subsection may be shared with other Federal agencies for the purpose of protecting national security.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security, or the designee of the Secretary, shall issue a notice of proposed rulemaking that will allow the Department of Homeland Security to compare passenger information for any international flight to or from the United States against the consolidated and integrated terrorist watchlist maintained by the Federal Government before departure of the flight.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security shall establish a timely and fair process for individuals identified as a threat under subparagraph (A) to appeal to the Department of Homeland Security the determination and correct any erroneous information.

children.heading: .—Records
children.label: ii .—Records
children.text: The process shall include the establishment of a method by which the Secretary of Homeland Security will be able to maintain a record of air passengers and other individuals who have been misidentified and have corrected erroneous information. To prevent repeated delays of misidentified passengers and other individuals, the Department of Homeland Security record shall contain information determined by the Secretary of Homeland Security to authenticate the identity of such a passenger or individual.
